title: varronia bullata subsp. humilis
intro: Varronia bullata subsp. humilis, also known as the small tree bush or bush varonia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is traditionally used in African and Caribbean medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function, although further scientific research is necessary to confirm these claims. Varronia bullata subsp. humilis is often prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, with its leaves and roots being the primary components used in herbal preparations.
